tools.name: account_get
tools.description: Retrieve the current Gorgias account's metadata: subdomain, plan, billing state, and account-level flags. Use this to confirm credentials are wired up, detect the tenant/subdomain in a multi-tenant agent, or branch logic on plan tier.

tools.name: account_settings-create
tools.description: Create a new account-level settings record for the current Gorgias tenant. Use when bootstrapping a fresh tenant or when an integration needs to seed default settings flags (locale, business hours, branding) that don't exist yet. Required: type. Optional: data.

params.name: type
params.description: example: business-hours

params.name: data
params.description: example: {'timezone': 'UTC', 'business_hours': {'days': '1,2,3,4,5', 'from_time': '09:...

tools.name: account_settings-list
tools.description: List the global settings on the current Gorgias account (business hours, language, default channels, notification preferences, etc.). Reach for this when an agent needs to honor tenant-wide configuration before composing a reply or routing a ticket.

tools.name: account_settings-update
tools.description: Update an account settings record by `id`. Use this to flip a tenant-wide flag, change business hours, or adjust a default channel — but only when the agent has authority to mutate account-level config (admin-grade operation). Required: id, type. Optional: data.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: type
params.description: example: business-hours

params.name: data
params.description: example: {'timezone': 'UTC', 'business_hours': {'days': '1,2,3,4,5', 'from_time': '09:...

tools.name: custom-fields_create
tools.description: Define a new custom field on tickets or customers (the only supported `object_type` values). Required body: `object_type`, `label`, `definition` (the type-specific settings, e.g. boolean/number/text and any picklist values live inside `definition`). Optional: `description`, `priority`, `required`, `external_id`. Use to extend the schema, not to set a value on an existing record.

params.name: object_type
params.description: Type of entity on which to use this custom field. (example: Ticket)

params.name: label
params.description: The name of the custom field. (example: Test field)

params.name: definition
params.description: The settings for this custom field, dependent on the data type. (example: {'input_settings': {'placeholder': 'Enter this here...'}})

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the custom field in a foreign system (ex: Zendesk). (example: custom-field-84203241)

params.name: description
params.description: The description of the custom field. (example: An amazing field description.)

params.name: priority
params.description: Order in which custom fields are displayed. (example: 1)

params.name: required
params.description: Whether this custom field is required. (example: True)

params.name: managed_type
params.description: The type of the managed field. (example: contact_reason)

params.name: deactivated_datetime
params.description: When the custom field was deactivated. (example: 2021-01-02T03:04:05.123456)

tools.name: custom-fields_get
tools.description: Fetch a single custom field definition by `id`, returning its data type, label, target object, and option list. Use to introspect schema before writing a value, especially to discover the legal enum options for picklist fields. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: custom-fields_list
tools.description: List custom field definitions for a single `object_type` (`Ticket` or `Customer` — REQUIRED query param). Optional: `search`, `archived`, `order_by`, `cursor`, `limit`. Call once per object type to discover what extensible schema exists before reading or writing values.

params.name: object_type
params.description: Filter to a single resource type: Ticket or Customer. REQUIRED by Gorgias — calls without it return 400.

params.name: search
params.description: Substring search across custom-field names.

params.name: archived
params.description: Include archived fields when true.

params.name: limit
params.description: Page size (Gorgias hard cap: 100).

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor from a prior response's meta.next_cursor.

params.name: order_by
params.description: Sort expression, e.g. 'created_datetime:desc'.

tools.name: custom-fields_update
tools.description: Update one custom field definition by `id` — relabel it, change its options, or toggle visibility. Note: this mutates the schema for every record that carries the field, so use sparingly and prefer per-record updates for one-off changes. Required: id. Optional: external_id, object_type, label (plus 6 more).

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the custom field in a foreign system (ex: Zendesk). (example: custom-field-84203241)

params.name: object_type
params.description: Type of entity on which to use this custom field. (example: Ticket)

params.name: label
params.description: The name of the custom field. (example: Test field)

params.name: description
params.description: The description of the custom field. (example: An amazing field description.)

params.name: priority
params.description: Order in which custom fields are displayed. (example: 1)

params.name: required
params.description: Whether this custom field is required. (example: True)

params.name: managed_type
params.description: The type of the managed field. (example: contact_reason)

params.name: definition
params.description: The settings for this custom field, dependent on the data type. (example: {'input_settings': {'placeholder': 'Enter this here...'}})

params.name: deactivated_datetime
params.description: When the custom field was deactivated. (example: 2021-01-02T03:04:05.123456)

tools.name: custom-fields_update-all
tools.description: Bulk-update multiple custom field definitions in one call (no path id). Useful when reordering picklist options or applying the same label change across several fields; avoid unless the agent really needs a batched schema edit.

tools.name: customers_create
tools.description: Create a new customer record. Pass `name`, `email`, optional `channels` (email/phone/social handles), and `data` for arbitrary key-value attributes. Use when an inbound message references a person who doesn't yet exist in Gorgias. Required: channels. Optional: email, external_id, language (plus 2 more).

params.name: channels
params.description: The [customer's contact channels](#the-customerchannel-object).

params.name: email
params.description: Primary email address of the customer. (example: example-email-value)

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the customer in a foreign system (Stripe, Aircall, etc.

params.name: language
params.description: The customer's preferred language (format: ISO_639-1).

params.name: name
params.description: Full name of the customer. (example: John Smith)

params.name: timezone
params.description: The customer's preferred timezone (format: IANA timezone name).

tools.name: customers_custom-fields-list
tools.description: List every custom field value attached to a single customer (`id`). Use to read CRM-style attributes (lifetime value, segment, loyalty tier) before deciding how to respond to or route a customer's ticket. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: customers_custom-fields-set
tools.description: Set a single custom field value on a customer: first `{id}` is the customer, second `{id}` is the custom field. Use for one-off mutations like flagging a VIP or recording a churn-risk score. Required: id, customer_id. Optional: value.

params.name: customer_id
params.description: customer_id

tools.name: customers_custom-fields-set-all
tools.description: Bulk-set custom field values on a single customer (`id`) — pass an array of field/value pairs. Preferred over the singular form when an agent needs to write several attributes at once after enriching a profile. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: customers_custom-fields-unset
tools.description: Clear a custom field value on a customer: first `{id}` is the customer ID, second `{id}` is the custom field ID. Use to unset (not delete) a per-customer attribute — the field definition itself remains. Required: customer_id, id. Destructive.

params.name: customer_id
params.description: customer_id

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: customers_data-update
tools.description: Set a customer's `data` blob (`id` in path). Body: `data` (required) plus optional `version` for last-write-wins (requests with an older version are ignored). Use when an integration pushes CRM context that should be displayed alongside the customer. Required: id, data. Optional: version.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: data
params.description: The customer data. (example: {'key': 'value'})

params.name: version
params.description: The date of the customer data. If we already have a more recent version, the request will be ignored. (example: 2022-05-13T12:34:21.918927)

tools.name: customers_delete
tools.description: Delete one customer by `id`. Hard-deletes the record and may cascade to associated tickets/messages depending on account settings — reach for this only when handling GDPR-style erasure requests or scrubbing test data. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: customers_delete-all
tools.description: Bulk-delete customers. Required body: `ids` (array of customer IDs to delete). Does NOT accept query-style filters — you must enumerate the IDs explicitly. Reserve for cleanup jobs or compliance erasure; destructive and irreversible. Required: ids.

params.name: ids
params.description: A list of customer IDs to delete.

tools.name: customers_get
tools.description: Fetch a single customer by `id`, including their channels (email, phone, social handles), `data` blob, and account-level attributes. Use after identifying a customer (e.g. via search) to load full profile context. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: customers_list
tools.description: List customers with pagination and optional filter params (`email`, `external_id`, `name`, `language`, `channel_type`, `channel_address`, `view_id`). All params are optional. The agent's primary way to look up a customer by email or external system ID before reading tickets or sending a reply. Optional: email, limit.

tools.name: customers_merge
tools.description: Merge one customer into another. Required query params: `source_id` (the duplicate, will be merged in and deleted) and `target_id` (the surviving customer). Body fields (channels, email, name, language, timezone, external_id) overwrite the target. Use when a duplicate is detected by email/phone; merges happen one pair at a time. Required: source_id, target_id. Optional: channels, email, external_id (plus 3 more).

params.name: source_id
params.description: The ID of the customer to merge (which will be merged into the targeted customer).

params.name: target_id
params.description: The ID of the targeted customer (which will still be here after the merge).

params.name: channels
params.description: The [customer's contact channels](#the-customerchannel-object).

params.name: email
params.description: Primary email address of the customer. (example: example-email-value)

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the customer in a foreign system (Stripe, Aircall, etc.

params.name: language
params.description: The customer's preferred language (format: ISO_639-1).

params.name: name
params.description: Full name of the customer. (example: John Smith)

params.name: timezone
params.description: The customer's preferred timezone (format: IANA timezone name).

tools.name: customers_update
tools.description: Update a customer (`id`) — change name, add/remove channels, edit external IDs, or overwrite top-level fields. Use after merging records, fixing a typo, or syncing data from an external CRM. Required: id. Optional: channels, email, external_id (plus 3 more).

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: channels
params.description: The [customer's contact channels](#the-customerchannel-object).

params.name: email
params.description: Primary email address of the customer. (example: example-email-value)

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the customer in a foreign system (Stripe, Aircall, etc.

params.name: language
params.description: The customer's preferred language (format: ISO_639-1).

params.name: name
params.description: Full name of the customer. (example: John Smith)

params.name: timezone
params.description: The customer's preferred timezone (format: IANA timezone name).

tools.name: events_get
tools.description: Retrieve a single audit event by `id` — captures who/what/when on ticket, customer, or settings mutations. Use to audit a specific change an agent or rule made, or to diagnose why a record looks the way it does. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: events_list
tools.description: List audit events. Documented filters: `object_type` (e.g. Ticket/Customer/User), `object_id`, `user_ids` (actor), `types` (event-type allowlist), `created_datetime` (with comparators), plus `cursor`/`limit`/`order_by`. Use to reconstruct a ticket's history or detect rule-driven changes.

tools.name: gorgias-jobs_create
tools.description: Kick off an asynchronous Gorgias job. Required body: `type` (enum: applyMacro, deleteTicket, exportTicket, importMacro, exportMacro, updateTicket, exportTicketDrilldown, exportConvertCampaignSalesDrilldown) and `params` (job-specific). Optional: `scheduled_datetime` (max 60 min in the future), `meta`. Poll with `gorgias-jobs_get` until the status leaves pending/running.

params.name: params
params.description: The parameters of the job. (example: {'ticket_ids': [1, 2, 3], 'updates': {'status': 'open'}})

params.name: type
params.description: The type of the job. Options: applyMacro, deleteTicket, exportTicket, importMacro, exportMacro, updateTicket, exportTicketDrilldown, exportConvertCampaignSalesDrilldown (enum: applyMacro, deleteTicket, exportTicket, importMacro, exportMacro, updateTicket, exportTicketDrilldown, exportConvertCampaignSalesDrilldown) (example: updateTicket)

params.name: meta
params.description: Metadata associated with the job. You can use this parameter to store structured information (key-value data) about the job. This field is not used by Gorgias. (example: {'description': 'This job closes all tickets created in 2018'})

params.name: scheduled_datetime
params.description: When the job was scheduled to be started. A job cannot be scheduled more than 60 minutes in the future. If unspecified, the job is queued for execution immediately after creation. (example: 2019-11-23T16:30:00.966927)

tools.name: gorgias-jobs_delete
tools.description: Delete a job record by `id`. Useful for cleaning up completed or failed entries from listings; does not cancel an in-flight job's side effects (the work it triggered may already be done). Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: gorgias-jobs_get
tools.description: Fetch a single async job (`id`) with its status, progress, params, and result/error fields. The polling endpoint after `gorgias-jobs_create` — loop on this until the job is no longer in `pending`/`running`. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: gorgias-jobs_list
tools.description: List async jobs with filters by type, status, and datetime. Use to find a recent export job by an agent or to surface stuck/failed jobs for retry.

tools.name: gorgias-jobs_update
tools.description: Update an async job (`id`) — typically to cancel it or adjust metadata. Reach for this only when you need to abort a long-running import/export rather than wait for it to complete. Required: id. Optional: meta, params, scheduled_datetime (plus 1 more).

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: meta
params.description: Metadata associated with the job. You can use this parameter to store structured information (key-value data) about the job. This field is not used by Gorgias. (example: {'description': 'This job closes all tickets created in 2018'})

params.name: params
params.description: The parameters of the job. (example: {'ticket_ids': [1, 2, 3], 'updates': {'status': 'open'}})

params.name: scheduled_datetime
params.description: When the job was scheduled to be started. A job cannot be scheduled more than 60 minutes in the future. If unspecified, the job is queued for execution immediately after creation. (example: 2019-11-23T16:30:00.966927)

params.name: status
params.description: The status of the job. Options: cancel_requested, canceled, done, errored, fatal_errored, pending, running, scheduled (enum: cancel_requested, canceled, done, errored, fatal_errored, pending, running, scheduled) (example: canceled)

tools.name: integrations_create
tools.description: Install a new third-party integration on the Gorgias account (Shopify, Instagram, SMS provider, etc.). Pass `type` and provider-specific credentials. Use when an agent is asked to connect a new channel or external system, not to send data to one. Required: name, type. Optional: deactivated_datetime, description, http (plus 1 more).

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the integration. Usually the email address, Facebook page name, etc. (example: My HTTP integration)

params.name: type
params.description: Type of integration. (enum: http) (example: http)

params.name: deactivated_datetime
params.description: When the integration was deactivated.

params.name: description
params.description: Description about the integration.

params.name: http
params.description: Only available for HTTP integrations, defines the configuration of the integration. (example: {'id': 987, 'url': 'https://company.com/api/customers?email={{ticket.customer...)

params.name: managed
params.description: Whether integration is managed by Gorgias.

tools.name: integrations_delete
tools.description: Uninstall an integration by `id`. Destructive — disconnects the channel and may stop syncing orders/messages from that source. Use only when the user explicitly wants to remove the connection. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: integrations_get
tools.description: Fetch a single integration (`id`) including its type, status, last-sync time, and provider-specific config. Use to verify an integration is healthy before relying on its data (e.g. orders, social DMs). Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: integrations_list
tools.description: List all installed integrations on the account — Shopify, Magento, Facebook, voice, etc. Use to discover what external systems an agent can pull context from before composing a reply.

tools.name: integrations_update
tools.description: Update an integration's config (`id`) — refresh credentials, toggle sync features, or rename. Reach for this when an integration is failing auth or needs a feature flag flipped. Required: id, name. Optional: deactivated_datetime, description, http.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the integration. Usually the email address, Facebook page name, etc. (example: My HTTP integration)

params.name: deactivated_datetime
params.description: When the integration was deactivated.

params.name: description
params.description: Description about the integration.

params.name: http
params.description: Only available for HTTP integrations, defines the configuration of the integration. (example: {'id': 987, 'url': 'https://company.com/api/customers?email={{ticket.customer...)

tools.name: macros_archive
tools.description: Archive one or more macros (soft delete) — pass macro IDs in the body. Use this rather than `macros_delete` to preserve history and make the macro reversible via `macros_unarchive`. Required: ids.

params.name: ids
params.description: List of macro ids to be archived

tools.name: macros_create
tools.description: Create a new macro: a reusable reply/action template. Required body: `name`. Optional: `intent`, `language`, `external_id`, and `actions` (array of action objects — this is where reply text, tag adds, status changes, etc. live, including any `{{variable}}` placeholders). Use to codify a common response so humans (and other agents) can apply it later.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the Macro. Tips: choose a name that can be easily searched. (example: Order status inquiry)

params.name: external_id
params.description: External ID of the macro in a foreign system. This field is not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it to your own value. (example: CUS-324512)

params.name: intent
params.description: The intention of the macro should be used for. (enum: discount/request, exchange/request, exchange/status, feedback, order/damaged, order/cancel, order/change, order/wrong) (example: discount/request)

params.name: language
params.description: The language of the macro in ISO 639-1 format. (example: en)

params.name: actions
params.description: A list of [actions](#the-macroaction-object) to be applied on tickets. (example: [{'arguments': {'tags': 'question, order-status'}, 'name': 'addTags', 'title'...)

tools.name: macros_delete
tools.description: Delete a macro by `id`. Hard-deletes it from the macro library. Prefer `macros_archive` for soft removal so historical references remain meaningful. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: macros_get
tools.description: Fetch a single macro by `id`, returning its body, actions, and variable definitions. Use before applying a macro so the agent can preview the rendered text and required variable values. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: macros_list
tools.description: List all macros, with optional filters (archived, name). The agent's discovery endpoint for available canned replies and bulk actions — useful before composing a reply from scratch.

tools.name: macros_unarchive
tools.description: Unarchive one or more macros, restoring them to the active library. Pass macro IDs in the body. The companion to `macros_archive`. Required: ids.

params.name: ids
params.description: List of macro ids to be unarchived

tools.name: macros_update
tools.description: Update a macro (`id`) — edit its body, variables, tags-to-add, or action list. Use when an agent is refining a canned reply or adjusting the side-effects it triggers. Required: id. Optional: external_id, name, intent (plus 2 more).

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: external_id
params.description: External ID of the macro in a foreign system. This field is not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it to your own value. (example: CUS-324512)

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the Macro. Tips: choose a name that can be easily searched. (example: Order status inquiry)

params.name: intent
params.description: The intention of the macro should be used for. (enum: discount/request, exchange/request, exchange/status, feedback, order/damaged, order/cancel, order/change, order/wrong) (example: discount/request)

params.name: language
params.description: The language of the macro in ISO 639-1 format. (example: en)

params.name: actions
params.description: A list of [actions](#the-macroaction-object) to be applied on tickets. (example: [{'arguments': {'tags': 'question, order-status'}, 'name': 'addTags', 'title'...)

tools.name: messages_list
tools.description: List messages account-wide, paginated. Supported filters are `ticket_id` only (plus `cursor`, `limit`, `order_by`); no channel/sender/datetime filtering at this endpoint. Prefer `tickets_messages-list` when you already know the ticket id.

tools.name: phone_call-events-get
tools.description: Fetch a single voice-call event by `id` — events capture call lifecycle (ringing, answered, hung-up, transferred). Use to drill into why a specific call ended a certain way. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: phone_call-events-list
tools.description: List voice-call lifecycle events. Documented filter is `call_id` only (plus `cursor`, `limit`). Use to inspect the event timeline for a specific call when debugging routing or hand-off behavior.

tools.name: phone_call-recordings-delete
tools.description: Delete a stored voice-call recording by `id`. Use to honor a customer privacy/erasure request or to scrub a test recording; the call metadata typically remains, only the audio blob is removed. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: phone_call-recordings-get
tools.description: Fetch metadata for a single call recording (`id`) — duration, URL, related call/ticket. Pair with `download_get_download` to retrieve the actual audio bytes. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: phone_call-recordings-list
tools.description: List voice-call recordings. Documented filter is `call_id` only (plus `cursor`, `limit`). Use to find the recording(s) attached to a specific call before reviewing or transcribing.

tools.name: phone_calls-get
tools.description: Fetch a single voice call (`id`) with direction, status, duration, participants, and the linked ticket. Use when an agent needs context on a specific phone interaction. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: phone_calls-list
tools.description: List voice calls, paginated. Documented filter is `ticket_id` only (plus `cursor`, `limit`, `order_by`). Use to surface calls linked to a specific ticket; richer filtering (direction/status/agent) is not exposed here.

tools.name: pickups_delete
tools.description: Delete a pickup record by `id`. Counterpart to `pickups_create_pickups`; use to cancel or remove a stale logistics pickup entry. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: reporting_stats
tools.description: Run a Gorgias analytics query: POST a JSON body with `metric`, `dimensions`, `filters`, and a `period`. The single endpoint for dashboards and report-style questions like 'tickets resolved by team last week' or 'first-response time by channel'. Required: query. Optional: cursor, limit.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Value indicating your position in the list of all analytics results. If omitted, the first analytics results of the list will be returned.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of analytics results to return.

params.name: query
params.description: The statistics query, dependent on the requested metric.

tools.name: rules_create
tools.description: Create a new automation rule. Required body: `name` and `code` (the rule logic written as JavaScript). Optional: `event_types` (e.g. ticket-created, ticket-updated), `priority`, `description`, `code_ast` (ESTree AST). Use to codify a workflow that the helpdesk evaluates on events; the conditions and actions are both expressed inside the JavaScript `code` string, not as a structured tree.

params.name: code
params.description: The logic of the rule (as JavaScript code). (example: if (eq(ticket.from_agent, false) && eq(ticket.status, 'open')) { if (con...)

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the rule. (example: Auto-close all tickets from Amazon)

params.name: code_ast
params.description: The logic of the rule (as an ESTree [AST](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_syntax_tree) representation). Generated from the code if not specified. (example: {'loc': {'start': {'line': 2, 'column': 0}, 'end': {'line': 10, 'column': 1}}...)

params.name: deactivated_datetime
params.description: When the rule was deactivated. (example: 2019-11-28T15:59:41.966927)

params.name: description
params.description: The description of the rule. (example: Automatically close all tickets from Amazon because there is nothing to do fo...)

params.name: event_types
params.description: A list of comma separated events that this rule will be executed on. (enum: ticket-created, ticket-updated, ticket-message-created, ticket-assigned, ticket-self-unsnoozed, satisfaction-survey-responded) (example: ticket-created,ticket-updated)

params.name: priority
params.description: Order of execution of the rule. Rules with higher priorities are executed first. (example: 100)

tools.name: rules_delete
tools.description: Delete an automation rule by `id`. Stops the rule from firing on future tickets but does not undo past actions. Use when retiring obsolete workflow logic. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: rules_get
tools.description: Fetch a single automation rule (`id`) with its full conditions/actions tree and enabled state. Use to inspect why a ticket was auto-modified or before tweaking rule logic. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: rules_list
tools.description: List all automation rules with their order, enabled state, and summary. The agent's map of what automations are currently shaping tickets, useful before adding new logic or diagnosing unexpected behavior.

tools.name: rules_set-priorities
tools.description: Set the execution priorities of automation rules. Required body: `priorities` — an array of objects mapping rule ids to their new priority value. Use after adding/reordering rules so they short-circuit in the intended order. Required: priorities. Optional: priorities.

params.name: priorities
params.description: A list of rule IDs with their new priorities.

tools.name: rules_update
tools.description: Update an automation rule (`id`) — edit conditions, actions, or enabled flag. Use to tune an existing workflow rather than deleting and recreating it. Required: id. Optional: code, code_ast, deactivated_datetime (plus 4 more).

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: code
params.description: The logic of the rule (as JavaScript code). (example: if (eq(ticket.from_agent, false) && eq(ticket.status, 'open')) { if (con...)

params.name: code_ast
params.description: The logic of the rule (as an ESTree [AST](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abstract_syntax_tree) representation). Generated from the code if not specified. (example: {'loc': {'start': {'line': 2, 'column': 0}, 'end': {'line': 10, 'column': 1}}...)

params.name: deactivated_datetime
params.description: When the rule was deactivated. (example: 2019-11-28T15:59:41.966927)

params.name: description
params.description: The description of the rule. (example: Automatically close all tickets from Amazon because there is nothing to do fo...)

params.name: event_types
params.description: A list of comma separated events that this rule will be executed on. (enum: ticket-created, ticket-updated, ticket-message-created, ticket-assigned, ticket-self-unsnoozed, satisfaction-survey-responded) (example: ticket-created,ticket-updated)

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the rule. (example: Auto-close all tickets from Amazon)

params.name: priority
params.description: Order of execution of the rule. Rules with higher priorities are executed first. (example: 100)

tools.name: satisfaction-surveys_create
tools.description: Create a satisfaction-survey instance attached to one ticket and customer. Required body: `customer_id`, `ticket_id`. Optional: `score` (1–5), `body_text` (customer comment), `should_send_datetime` (set to `null` to skip sending). Use to manually attach or backfill a CSAT result on a ticket — NOT to define a reusable survey template (Gorgias's CSAT templates are managed via integrations/settings, not this endpoint).

params.name: customer_id
params.description: The ID of the customer who filled the survey. (example: 120)

params.name: ticket_id
params.description: The ID of the ticket the survey is associated with. (example: 12)

params.name: body_text
params.description: The comment sent by the customer. (example: Such speed, much pertinent.)

params.name: created_datetime
params.description: When the survey was created. (example: 2019-11-16T15:59:41.966927)

params.name: meta
params.description: Data associated with the satisfaction survey. You can use this parameter to store structured information (key-value data) about the satisfaction survey. This field is not used by Gorgias. (example: {'externalData': {'id': 1234}})

params.name: score
params.description: The level of satisfaction. Scores range from 1 to 5. (example: 2)

params.name: scored_datetime
params.description: When the survey was filled by the customer. (example: 2019-11-25T15:59:41.966927)

params.name: sent_datetime
params.description: When the survey was sent. If is not set it means that it was not sent yet. (example: 2019-11-23T15:59:41.966927)

params.name: should_send_datetime
params.description: When the survey should be sent. If not set then the survey will not be sent by Gorgias. (example: 2019-11-23T15:59:41.966927)

tools.name: satisfaction-surveys_get
tools.description: Fetch a single satisfaction-survey instance by `id` — the linked ticket/customer, score, customer comment, and send/score timestamps. Use to read the CSAT result for a specific ticket. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: satisfaction-surveys_list
tools.description: List satisfaction-survey instances (each one tied to a single ticket). Filter with `ticket_id` to fetch the survey for one ticket, or paginate via `cursor`/`limit`. Use for CSAT reporting and to surface scores per ticket.

tools.name: satisfaction-surveys_update
tools.description: Update a satisfaction-survey instance (`id`) — typically to record/correct the customer's `score` (1–5), `body_text`, or to schedule/cancel its send via `should_send_datetime`. There is no template wording or trigger logic at this endpoint. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: body_text
params.description: The comment sent by the customer. (example: Such speed, much pertinent.)

params.name: created_datetime
params.description: When the survey was created. (example: 2019-11-16T15:59:41.966927)

params.name: customer_id
params.description: The ID of the customer who filled the survey. (example: 120)

params.name: meta
params.description: Data associated with the satisfaction survey. You can use this parameter to store structured information (key-value data) about the satisfaction survey. This field is not used by Gorgias. (example: {'externalData': {'id': 1234}})

params.name: score
params.description: The level of satisfaction. Scores range from 1 to 5. (example: 2)

params.name: scored_datetime
params.description: When the survey was filled by the customer. (example: 2019-11-25T15:59:41.966927)

params.name: sent_datetime
params.description: When the survey was sent. If is not set it means that it was not sent yet. (example: 2019-11-23T15:59:41.966927)

params.name: should_send_datetime
params.description: When the survey should be sent. If not set then the survey will not be sent by Gorgias. (example: 2019-11-23T15:59:41.966927)

params.name: ticket_id
params.description: The ID of the ticket the survey is associated with. (example: 12)

tools.name: tags_create
tools.description: Create a new tag in the account's tag library. Body: `name` (required, max 256 chars, case-sensitive), `description` (optional, max 1024), `decoration` (optional, styling object). Use when the agent needs a new label that doesn't exist yet — prefer reusing an existing tag where possible. Required: name. Optional: decoration, description.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the tag. Tags' names are case sensitive. (example: urgent)

params.name: decoration
params.description: Information related to the style of the tag.

params.name: description
params.description: Short description of the tag. (example: Mark a ticket as urgent)

tools.name: tags_delete
tools.description: Delete a tag by `id`. Removes it from the library and unassociates it from every ticket/customer that carries it. Destructive — prefer merging into another tag if you want to preserve history. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tags_delete-all
tools.description: Bulk-delete tags. Required body: `ids` (array of tag IDs, min 1). Tags currently referenced by macros or rules cannot be deleted, and views using them will be deactivated. Reserve for housekeeping; destructive. Required: ids.

params.name: ids
params.description: IDs of the tags to delete (example: [123, 456, 789])

tools.name: tags_get
tools.description: Fetch a single tag (`id`) with its name, decoration, and metadata. Use to verify a tag exists before applying it, or to read its display style. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tags_list
tools.description: List all tags in the account, optionally filtered by name. The agent's lookup endpoint for finding the right existing tag (and avoiding duplicates) before tagging a ticket or customer.

tools.name: tags_merge
tools.description: Merge other tags INTO this tag — path `{id}` is the destination (surviving) tag, and the body field `source_tags_ids` (array of integer IDs) lists the source tags to be merged and deleted. Use to consolidate duplicate or near-duplicate labels. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: source_tags_ids
params.description: IDs of the tags that will be merged into the destination tag. (example: [123, 456, 789])

tools.name: tags_update
tools.description: Update a tag (`id`) — rename it or change its color/decoration. Affects every record currently carrying the tag, so use carefully on widely-applied labels. Required: id. Optional: name, description, decoration.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the tag. Tags' names are case sensitive. (example: urgent)

params.name: description
params.description: Short description of the tag. (example: Mark a ticket as urgent)

params.name: decoration
params.description: Information related to the style of the tag.

tools.name: teams_create
tools.description: Create a new team (group of agents) in the account. Pass `name` and optionally members. Use when organizing routing or stats by squad — e.g. 'Tier-1 Support', 'Billing'. Required: name. Optional: description, decoration, members.

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the team. (example: Warehouse team)

params.name: description
params.description: Longer description of the team.

params.name: decoration
params.description: Object describing how the team appears on the webpage. Currently only supports `emoji` field that shows before the team's name.

params.name: members
params.description: The list of users within the team.

tools.name: teams_delete
tools.description: Delete a team by `id`. Removes it from routing rules and views; members remain but lose the team grouping. Use when retiring a squad structure. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: teams_get
tools.description: Fetch a single team (`id`) with its members and metadata. Use when an agent needs to know who's on a team before assigning or escalating a ticket. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: teams_list
tools.description: List all teams in the account. The agent's lookup for valid team IDs/names when assigning a ticket, routing via a rule, or filtering reports.

tools.name: teams_update
tools.description: Update a team (`id`) — rename it or change its membership. Use to reorganize agents or correct a misconfigured team. Required: id. Optional: name, description, decoration (plus 1 more).

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: name
params.description: Name of the team. (example: Warehouse team)

params.name: description
params.description: Longer description of the team.

params.name: decoration
params.description: Object describing how the team appears on the webpage. Currently only supports `emoji` field that shows before the team's name.

params.name: members
params.description: The list of users within the team.

tools.name: ticket-search_query
tools.description: Full-text search across Gorgias tickets, customers, and messages. POST a JSON body with `query`, `resource_type`, and optional `filters`. The agent-friendly path to find a prior conversation by topic or customer email when you only have natural-language search terms. Required: type. Optional: query, size.

params.name: type
params.description: The type of search. Supported types: (enum: agent, customer, customer_profile, customer_channel, customer_channel_email, customer_channel_phone, customers_by_phone, integration) (example: agent)

params.name: query
params.description: Text query used to search for resources. (example: customer-lookup-placeholder)

params.name: size
params.description: Maximum number of results returned. (example: 10)

tools.name: tickets_create
tools.description: Create a new ticket. Body specifies `channel`, `via`, `subject`, an initial `messages` array, the customer, and optional assignee/tags/status. Use when the agent is opening a fresh conversation rather than replying to an existing one. Required: messages. Optional: assignee_team, assignee_user, channel (plus 22 more).

params.name: messages
params.description: Messages of the ticket

params.name: assignee_team
params.description: Team assigned to the ticket (example: {})

params.name: assignee_user
params.description: User assigned to this ticket (example: {})

params.name: channel
params.description: Channel used to initiate the conversation with the customer (example: email)

params.name: closed_datetime
params.description: When the ticket was closed

params.name: created_datetime
params.description: When the ticket was created

params.name: custom_fields
params.description: Custom fields associated with the ticket

params.name: customer
params.description: Customer associated with the ticket

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the ticket in a foreign system. This field is not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it as you wish.

params.name: from_agent
params.description: Whether the first message of the ticket was sent by your company to a customer, or the opposite

params.name: language
params.description: Language primarily used in the ticket. The language is automatically detected on the first messages by Gorgias if not set explicitly. Once the language has been set, it won't be updated according to future messages.

params.name: last_message_datetime
params.description: When the last message was sent

params.name: last_received_message_datetime
params.description: When the last customer's message was received

params.name: meta
params.description: Metadata associated with the ticketYou can use this field to store structured information (key-value data) about the ticket

params.name: opened_datetime
params.description: When the ticket was opened for the first time by a User

params.name: priority
params.description: Priority of the ticket

params.name: snooze_datetime
params.description: When the ticket will be re-opened automatically

params.name: spam
params.description: Whether the ticket is considered as spam

params.name: split_from
params.description: ID of the ticket that this ticket was split from.

params.name: status
params.description: The status of the ticket

params.name: subject
params.description: Subject of the ticket

params.name: tags
params.description: The tags associated with the ticket

params.name: trashed_datetime
params.description: When the ticket was moved to the trash

params.name: updated_datetime
params.description: When the ticket was lastly updated

params.name: via
params.description: How the first message of the ticket has been received or sent from Gorgias (enum: aircall, api, chat, contact_form, email, facebook, facebook-mention, facebook-messenger)

params.name: imported
params.description: Whether the ticket was created by a historical import. When set to true, the ticket and all its messages are flagged as imported.

tools.name: tickets_custom-fields-list
tools.description: List every custom field value on ticket (`id`). Use to read structured metadata an agent or integration attached (e.g. RMA number, refund reason) before composing context-aware replies. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_custom-fields-set
tools.description: Set a single custom field value on a ticket: first `{id}` is the ticket, second `{id}` is the custom field. Use to write a structured attribute (e.g. refund amount, order number) onto a specific ticket. Required: id, ticket_id. Optional: value.

params.name: ticket_id
params.description: ticket_id

tools.name: tickets_custom-fields-set-all
tools.description: Bulk-set custom field values on ticket (`id`) — pass an array of field/value pairs. Preferred when an agent needs to write several structured attributes at once after triaging a ticket. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_custom-fields-unset
tools.description: Clear a custom field value on a ticket: first `{id}` is the ticket, second `{id}` is the custom field. Unsets (does not delete the schema) — use to revert an incorrectly auto-populated value. Required: ticket_id, id. Destructive.

params.name: ticket_id
params.description: ticket_id

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_delete
tools.description: Delete a ticket by `id`. Hard-deletes the conversation and its messages — reserve for GDPR erasure, spam, or accidental tickets. Most workflows prefer setting status to `closed` instead. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_get
tools.description: Fetch a single ticket by `id` with status, channel, assignee, customer, tags, and summary fields. Use after identifying a ticket (via search/list) to load core context before reading messages or replying. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_list
tools.description: List tickets with filters (status, assignee, customer, channel, datetime, tag). The agent's primary endpoint for queue scans like 'open tickets assigned to me' or 'all tickets for customer X'; prefer search for natural-language queries. Optional: customer_id, limit.

tools.name: tickets_messages-create
tools.description: Post a new message on ticket (`id`) — used to reply to the customer or write an internal note. The body distinguishes public ('outgoing' to channel) from internal ('internal-note') via fields like `sender`, `channel`, `via`, and `body_html`/`body_text`. Reach for this when the agent decides to respond. Required: id, channel, from_agent. Optional: action, actions, attachments (plus 25 more).

params.name: action
params.description: Policy applied on external actions associated with the message if they failed.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: channel
params.description: Channel used to send the message

params.name: from_agent
params.description: Whether the message was sent by your company

params.name: actions
params.description: Actions associated with the message

params.name: attachments
params.description: Attachments associated with the message

params.name: body_html
params.description: HTML version of the body of the message

params.name: body_text
params.description: Text version of the body of the message

params.name: created_datetime
params.description: When the message was created

params.name: deleted_datetime
params.description: When the message was deleted

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the message in a foreign system (Aircall, Zendesk, etc...). This field is not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it as you wish.

params.name: failed_datetime
params.description: When the message failed to be sent. Messages that couldn't be sent can be resent.

params.name: headers
params.description: Headers of the message

params.name: integration_id
params.description: ID of the Integration used to send the message

params.name: last_sending_error
params.description: Details of the last error encountered when Gorgias attempted to send the message

params.name: macros
params.description: Macros used to compose the message

params.name: message_id
params.description: ID of the message on the service that sent the message. It can be the ID of an email, a Messenger message, a Facebook comment, etc...

params.name: meta
params.description: Metadata associated with the ticket message. You can use this field to store structured information (key-value data) about the ticket message.

params.name: opened_datetime
params.description: When the message was seen by its recipient

params.name: public
params.description: Whether the message is public. Only internal notes are private.

params.name: sent_datetime
params.description: When the message was sent. If omitted, the message will be sent by Gorgias.

params.name: source
params.description: Information used to route the message. It contains the names and the addresses of the sender and receivers.

params.name: stripped_html
params.description: HTML version of the body of the message without email signatures and previous replies.

params.name: stripped_signature
params.description: Signature stripped from the body of the message

params.name: stripped_text
params.description: Text version of the body of the message without email signatures and previous replies.

params.name: subject
params.description: Subject of the message

params.name: via
params.description: How the message has been received or sent from Gorgias (enum: aircall, api, chat, contact_form, email, facebook, facebook-mention, facebook-messenger)

params.name: imported
params.description: Whether the message was created by a historical import.

params.name: mention_ids
params.description: List of User IDs to mention along with the internal note.

params.name: receiver
params.description: Primary receiver of the message. It can be a user or a customer. Optional when the source type is 'internal-note'.

params.name: sender
params.description: Person who sent the message. It can be a user or a customer.

tools.name: tickets_messages-delete
tools.description: Delete a message from a ticket: first `{id}` is the ticket, second `{id}` is the message. Use sparingly — typically only to scrub a message containing sensitive data or an accidental send. Required: ticket_id, id. Destructive.

params.name: ticket_id
params.description: ticket_id

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_messages-get
tools.description: Fetch a single message: first `{id}` is the ticket, second `{id}` is the message. Use to load full body and attachment details for a specific entry without pulling the whole thread. Required: ticket_id, id.

params.name: ticket_id
params.description: ticket_id

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_messages-list
tools.description: List all messages on ticket (`id`) in chronological order — both customer-sent and agent-sent, public and internal notes. The agent's primary read endpoint for understanding the full conversation context. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_messages-update
tools.description: Update a message: first `{id}` is the ticket, second `{id}` is the message. Typically used to edit an internal note's body or correct a draft; channel-delivered messages may be immutable depending on channel. Required: ticket_id, id, channel, from_agent. Optional: action, attachments, body_html (plus 10 more).

params.name: action
params.description: Policy applied on external actions associated with the message if they failed.

params.name: ticket_id
params.description: ticket_id

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: channel
params.description: The channel used to send the message. (enum: aircall, api, chat, contact_form, email, facebook, facebook-mention, facebook-messenger) (example: email)

params.name: from_agent
params.description: Whether the message was sent by your company to a customer, or the opposite. (example: True)

params.name: attachments
params.description: A list of files attached to the message. (example: [])

params.name: body_html
params.description:
The full HTML version of the body of the message, if any. (example: Hello,

I can't place an order on your site, it says: I don't...)

params.name: body_text
params.description: The full text version of the body of the message, if any. (example: Hello, I can't place an order on your site, it says: I don't have en...)

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the message in a foreign system (Aircall, Zendesk, etc...). This field is not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it as you wish. (example: MSG-78545)

params.name: failed_datetime
params.description: When the message failed to be sent. Messages that couldn't be sent can be resend.

params.name: message_id
params.description: ID of the message on the service that send the message.It can be the ID of an email, a Messenger message, a Facebook comment, etc... (example: <123345676453.2445.234@web>)

params.name: receiver
params.description: The primary receiver of the message. It can be a user or a customer. Optional when the source type is 'internal-note'. (example: {'id': 8})

params.name: sender
params.description: The person who sent the message. It can be a user or a customer. (example: {'id': 93})

params.name: sent_datetime
params.description: When the message was sent. If ommited, the message will be sent by Gorgias.

params.name: source
params.description: Information used to route the message. It contains the names and the addresses of the sender and receivers. (example: {'type': 'email', 'from': {'id': 7, 'name': 'Sender Doe', 'address': 'sender@...)

params.name: subject
params.description: The subject of the message. (example: Re:Refund request)

params.name: via
params.description: How the message has been received, or sent from Gorgias. (enum: aircall, api, chat, contact_form, email, facebook, facebook-mention, facebook-messenger) (example: api)

tools.name: tickets_tags-add
tools.description: Add one or more tags to ticket (`id`). The body shape (tag IDs vs names; whether unknown names auto-create) is not confirmed against the dev docs — verify before relying on auto-create behavior. Use to categorize a ticket once the agent identifies its topic. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: names
params.description: The names of the tags. (example: ['refund', 'billing'])

params.name: ids
params.description: The IDs of the tags. (example: [12, 34])

tools.name: tickets_tags-list
tools.description: List the tags currently attached to ticket (`id`). Use to read the ticket's categorization before deciding what additional tags to add or what rule branch to take. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_tags-remove
tools.description: Remove tags from ticket (`id`). Pass the tag IDs/names to detach. Use when re-categorizing a ticket or undoing an over-eager auto-tag. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: tickets_tags-replace
tools.description: Replace ticket (`id`)'s entire tag set with the supplied list. Use for full re-tagging; for additive/subtractive changes prefer the `create_tags` / `delete_tags` ticket endpoints. Required: id. Optional: names, ids.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: names
params.description: The names of the tags. (example: ['refund', 'billing'])

params.name: ids
params.description: The IDs of the tags. (example: [12, 34])

tools.description: Update a ticket (`id`) — change status (`open`/`closed`/`resolved`), assignee, priority, subject, or `via`. The workhorse for state transitions: closing after resolution, reassigning to a team, escalating priority. Required: id. Optional: assignee_team, assignee_user, channel (plus 20 more).

params.name: external_id

params.name: is_unread

tools.name: users_create
tools.description: Create a new user (Gorgias agent/operator). Pass name, email, role, and optionally team memberships. Use when provisioning a new support agent — typically an admin-only operation. Required: email, name, role. Optional: bio, country, external_id (plus 3 more).

params.name: email
params.description: Email address of the user. `password_confirmation` field must be provided to change the email. (example: staff-email-placeholder)

params.name: name
params.description: Full name of the user. (example: Steve Frazelli)

params.name: role
params.description: The role of the user. (example: {'name': 'admin'})

params.name: bio
params.description: Short biography of the user. (example: Full stack developer at Gorgias)

params.name: country
params.description: Country of the user (example: FR)

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the user in a foreign system (Stripe, Aircall, etc...). This field is not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it as you wish. (example: user-84203241)

params.name: language
params.description: (enum: fr, en)

params.name: meta
params.description: Data associated with the user. You can use this field to store structured information (key-value data) about the user. (example: {'avatar_url': 'https://avatars.example.com/steve-profile.jpeg', 'position': ...)

params.name: timezone
params.description: (enum: Africa/Abidjan, Africa/Accra, Africa/Addis_Ababa, Africa/Algiers, Africa/Asmara, Africa/Asmera, Africa/Bamako, Africa/Bangui)

tools.name: users_delete
tools.description: Delete a user (`id`) — deactivates the agent account and removes them from routing. Their historical ticket activity remains. Reach for this when an agent leaves the org. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: users_get
tools.description: Fetch a single user (`id`) — agent name, email, role, teams, status. Use to look up who an assignee is or to verify a user's role before assigning sensitive tickets. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: users_list
tools.description: List users (Gorgias agents/operators) on the account, with filters for role, status, and team. The agent's lookup endpoint for valid assignee IDs before reassigning or routing a ticket.

tools.name: users_update
tools.description: Update a user (`id`) — change role, name, team membership, or active state. Use for admin operations like promoting an agent to lead or moving someone between teams. Required: id. Optional: bio, country, email (plus 10 more).

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: bio
params.description: Short biography of the user. (example: Full stack developer at Gorgias)

params.name: country
params.description: Country of the user (example: FR)

params.name: email
params.description: Email address of the user. `password_confirmation` field must be provided to change the email. (example: staff-email-placeholder)

params.name: external_id
params.description: ID of the user in a foreign system (Stripe, Aircall, etc...). This field is not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it as you wish. (example: user-84203241)

params.name: language
params.description: Language of the user. (enum: fr, en) (example: fr)

params.name: meta
params.description: Data associated with the user. You can use this field to store structured information (key-value data) about the user. (example: {'avatar_url': 'https://avatars.example.com/steve-profile.jpeg', 'position': ...)

params.name: name
params.description: Full name of the user. (example: Steve Frazelli)

params.name: new_password
params.description: New password of the user. `old_password` field must be provided when changing the password. (example: NewPassword6502!)

params.name: old_password
params.description: Current password of the user. (example: OldPassword5031!)

params.name: password_confirmation
params.description: Current password of the user. (example: OldPassword5031!)

params.name: role
params.description: The role of the user. (example: {'name': 'admin'})

params.name: timezone
params.description: Timezone of the user. (enum: Africa/Abidjan, Africa/Accra, Africa/Addis_Ababa, Africa/Algiers, Africa/Asmara, Africa/Asmera, Africa/Bamako, Africa/Bangui) (example: US/Pacific)

tools.name: views_create
tools.description: Create a saved view — a filtered ticket list (e.g. 'My open tickets', 'Urgent + unassigned') defined by status/tag/assignee/channel criteria. Use when an agent is asked to materialize a recurring query as a reusable filter. Required: slug. Optional: decoration, fields, filters (plus 7 more).

params.name: slug
params.description: DEPRECATED - URL-compatible name of the view. (example: my-tickets)

params.name: decoration
params.description: Object describing how the view appears in our applications. Currently only supports `emoji` field that shows before the view's name. (example: {'emoji': '🙏'})

params.name: fields
params.description: List of object's attributes to be displayed in the UI of our applications. (example: ['id', 'details', 'tags'])

params.name: filters
params.description: The logic used to filter the items to be displayed in the view (as JavaScript code). (example: eq(ticket.assignee_user.id, '{{current_user.id}}') && eq(ticket.status, 'open'))

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the view. (example: My view)

params.name: order_by
params.description: Name of the object's attribute used to sort the items of the view. (example: updated_datetime)

params.name: order_dir
params.description: Sort direction of the items displayed in the view. Options: `asc` or `desc`. (enum: asc, desc) (example: desc)

params.name: shared_with_teams
params.description: IDs of teams this view is shared with. (example: [1, 2])

params.name: shared_with_users
params.description: IDs of users this view is shared with. (example: [1, 2])

params.name: type
params.description: Type of objects the view is applied on. (enum: ticket-list) (example: ticket-list)

params.name: visibility
params.description: Visibility of the view. (example: public)

tools.name: views_delete
tools.description: Delete a saved view by `id`. Removes it from the sidebar for everyone who saw it. Use when retiring stale filters. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: views_get
tools.description: Fetch a single saved view (`id`) with its filter definition and metadata. Use to introspect what conditions a view encodes before relying on its results or editing it. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: views_items-list
tools.description: Return the ticket items currently matching saved view (`id`). Required: `id` (path). Optional: `cursor`, `direction` (prev/next), `limit` (1-100, default 30), `order_by`, `ignored_item`. Use to read 'what's in this view right now' rather than re-deriving the filter manually.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: views_items-update
tools.description: Update the materialized items of a view (`id`) — used to reorder, bulk-mutate, or refresh the cached set depending on view type. Niche; most agents read items rather than mutating them. Required: id. Optional: cursor, direction, ignored_item (plus 3 more).

params.name: cursor
params.description: Value indicating your position in the list of all the view's items. If omitted, the first items of the list will be returned.

params.name: direction
params.description: - `prev` returns items before the cursor.

params.name: ignored_item
params.description: ID of an item to ignore in the list of returned items.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return.

params.name: order_by
params.description: Attribute used to order views.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: view
params.description: example: {'id': 123, 'category': 'user', 'created_datetime': '2019-11-23T15:59:41.9669...

tools.name: views_list
tools.description: List all saved views on the account, including ownership and visibility. The agent's catalogue of pre-built ticket filters available for navigation or reporting.

tools.name: views_update
tools.description: Update a saved view (`id`) — change its filter criteria, name, or sharing. Use to evolve a view's definition as triage workflows change. Required: id. Optional: decoration, fields, filters (plus 8 more).

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: decoration
params.description: Object describing how the view appears in our applications. Currently only supports `emoji` field that shows before the view's name. (example: {'emoji': '🙏'})

params.name: fields
params.description: List of object's attributes to be displayed in the UI of our applications. (example: ['id', 'details', 'tags'])

params.name: filters
params.description: The logic used to filter the items to be displayed in the view (as JavaScript code). (example: eq(ticket.assignee_user.id, '{{current_user.id}}') && eq(ticket.status, 'open'))

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the view. (example: My view)

params.name: order_by
params.description: Name of the object's attribute used to sort the items of the view. (example: updated_datetime)

params.name: order_dir
params.description: Sort direction of the items displayed in the view. Options: `asc` or `desc`. (enum: asc, desc) (example: desc)

params.name: shared_with_teams
params.description: IDs of teams this view is shared with. (example: [1, 2])

params.name: shared_with_users
params.description: IDs of users this view is shared with. (example: [1, 2])

params.name: slug
params.description: DEPRECATED - URL-compatible name of the view. (example: my-tickets)

params.name: type
params.description: Type of objects the view is applied on. (enum: ticket-list) (example: ticket-list)

params.name: visibility
params.description: Visibility of the view. (example: public)

tools.name: widgets_create
tools.description: Create a new agent-facing sidebar widget shown inside the Gorgias helpdesk (on ticket, customer, or user views — set by `context`). Required: `template` (defines the data source, e.g. Shopify, Stripe, HTTP). Optional: `order`, `integration_id` (required for HTTP), `app_id`, `deactivated_datetime`. This is NOT a customer-facing chat widget — those are managed via the Gorgias Chat integration.

params.name: template
params.description: Template to render the data of the widget. (example: {'type': 'wrapper', 'widgets': [{'path': 'account_info', 'type': 'card', 'tit...)

params.name: type
params.description: Type of data the widget is attached to. (enum: bigcommerce, custom, customer_external_data, http, magento2, recharge, shopify, smile) (example: http)

params.name: context
params.description: The context to display this widget in. (enum: ticket, customer, user) (example: ticket)

params.name: deactivated_datetime
params.description: When the widget was deactivated. (example: 2020-12-03T13:00:00.123456)

params.name: integration_id
params.description: ID of the integration that the widget's data is attached to. Only used for widgets attached to HTTP integrations. Widgets are the same for all Shopify integrations, but each HTTP integration needs a specific widget.

params.name: app_id
params.description: The ID of the 3rd party app that the widget's data is attached to. Used for customer external data widgets. (example: 60b9f144096cf92533e0a14d)

params.name: order
params.description: Order of precedence of the widget. Widgets with lower order are shown first. (example: 3)

tools.name: widgets_delete
tools.description: Delete a sidebar widget config by `id`. After deletion the widget stops rendering in the helpdesk UI on the configured `context` (ticket/customer/user). Coordinate with the team relying on the data surface. Required: id. Destructive.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: widgets_get
tools.description: Fetch a single sidebar widget (`id`) with its `context` (ticket/customer/user), `template` (data source), order, and integration linkage. Use to introspect a helpdesk sidebar widget before editing it. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

tools.name: widgets_list
tools.description: List all agent-facing sidebar widgets on the account, optionally filtered by `integration_id` or `app_id`. Use to discover which Shopify/Stripe/HTTP-backed sidebar surfaces exist on ticket/customer/user views before editing one. NOT a chat-widget listing.

tools.name: widgets_update
tools.description: Update a sidebar widget (`id`) — typically to change its `template` (data source), `order` (display position), `context`, or to deactivate it via `deactivated_datetime`. Affects what agents see in the helpdesk sidebar. NOT a customer-facing chat widget. Required: id.

params.name: id
params.description: id

params.name: context
params.description: The context to display this widget in. (enum: ticket, customer, user) (example: ticket)

params.name: deactivated_datetime
params.description: When the widget was deactivated. (example: 2020-12-03T13:00:00.123456)

params.name: integration_id
params.description: ID of the integration that the widget's data is attached to. Only used for widgets attached to HTTP integrations. Widgets are the same for all Shopify integrations, but each HTTP integration needs a specific widget.

params.name: app_id
params.description: The ID of the 3rd party app that the widget's data is attached to. Used for customer external data widgets. (example: 60b9f144096cf92533e0a14d)

params.name: order
params.description: Order of precedence of the widget. Widgets with lower order are shown first. (example: 3)

params.name: template
params.description: Template to render the data of the widget. (example: {'type': 'wrapper', 'widgets': [{'path': 'account_info', 'type': 'card', 'tit...)

params.name: type
params.description: Type of data the widget is attached to. (enum: bigcommerce, custom, customer_external_data, http, magento2, recharge, shopify, smile) (example: http)
